description: A one-way ticket for a single journey between an origin and destination station. Valid on the selected travel date only.

metro.validity: Travel date only. Ticket expires at midnight on the travel date.
metro.refund_condition: Refundable only before first tap-in or journey commencement under RF003.

national_rail.formula: amount = base_fare_usd + (stops_travelled × per_stop_rate_usd), rates vary by fare_class and service_type
national_rail.validity: Selected travel date and scheduled service only. Not valid on a different service without a change fee.
national_rail.change_deadline: At least 24 hours before scheduled departure
national_rail.refund_condition: Refundable according to RF001 or RF002 cancellation windows, or under disruption/payment-error policies when applicable.

usage_rules.qr_code_use: Each single ticket QR code may be used for one completed journey only.
usage_rules.same_day_only: Metro single tickets expire at 23:59 on the travel date. National rail single tickets are valid only for the booked service unless changed under booking rules.
usage_rules.interchange: Changing between metro and national rail requires a valid ticket for each network unless the passenger has separately purchased both tickets.

description: A two-leg ticket covering an outbound journey and a return journey on the same route. Each leg is priced and treated independently.

national_rail.formula: total amount = outbound_fare + return_fare (each leg calculated separately using stops-based pricing for the booked fare class)
national_rail.outbound_validity: Selected outbound travel date and scheduled service.
national_rail.return_validity: Any scheduled service on the same route within 30 days of the outbound travel date.
national_rail.change_deadline: At least 24 hours before the leg being changed
national_rail.refund_rule: RF001 (normal service), RF002 (express service). Each leg is assessed separately.
national_rail.notes: If the outbound leg has already been travelled, only the return leg is eligible for cancellation and refund.
national_rail.refund_condition: Each leg is assessed separately under RF001 or RF002. After the outbound leg is completed, only the unused return leg may be refunded or changed.
national_rail.general_validity_exception: The return leg is an exception to the general same-service validity rule: it may be used on any scheduled service on the same route within 30 days of the outbound travel date, subject to the return usage rules.

usage_rules.outbound_and_return: A return ticket contains two journey legs. The return leg may only be travelled after the outbound leg has been completed unless customer service authorises an exception during disruptions.
usage_rules.partial_use: Refunds and changes are assessed per leg. A passenger may cancel an unused leg if it meets the relevant deadline and policy conditions.
usage_rules.same_route_only: The return leg must use the same origin and destination pair in reverse direction.

description: Unlimited travel on all metro lines (M1, M2, M3, M4) for one full calendar day. Not valid on national rail services.

metro.validity: Calendar day of purchase from 00:00 to 23:59. Cannot be used on a different date.
metro.notes: A day pass offers better value than individual single tickets for passengers making 3 or more metro trips in a day. Tap in and out normally at each station.
metro.refund_condition: Refundable only before first tap-in under RF004, except when disruption or natural disaster policies apply.

usage_rules.activation: A metro day pass is activated at the first tap-in on the purchase date.
usage_rules.unlimited_use: After activation, the passenger may make unlimited metro journeys until 23:59 on the same calendar day.
usage_rules.not_valid_on_national_rail: Metro day passes cannot be used on national rail services, even at interchange stations.
usage_rules.single_user_only: A day pass is non-transferable and may only be used by the passenger who purchased it.
usage_rules.carry_over: Unused validity does not carry over to the next calendar day regardless of activation time.
usage_rules.partial_refund_limit: Once activated, a metro day pass is not normally eligible for partial refund because unlimited usage makes the unused portion non-identifiable; natural-disaster exceptions are handled under RF009.
